# RQ-0956 Execution Directive

## Task Goal
Fix follow-up iteration planning behavior for multi-iteration, multi-phase runs so **Phase 1 of iteration 2+ does not re-run a global clean-repo assertion** that conflicts with expected dirty state from iteration 1 implementation work.

## Why This Change Is Required
Current Phase 1 enforcement captures baseline dirty paths/snapshots when `allow_dirty_repo` is true, but it still always runs `git::require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths(...)` in the enforcement loop. In multi-iteration (e.g., `iterations=2`, `phases=3`) runs, follow-up iterations are expected to inherit dirty implementation files from prior iterations. We must keep plan-only protections while avoiding a redundant/incorrect follow-up global clean assertion.

## Ground Truth in Current Code
- Follow-up iterations are identified in `execute_iteration_phases` via `is_followup = iteration_index > 1`; this currently only influences `allow_dirty` and prompt context.
- `PhaseInvocation` has `allow_dirty_repo` and `is_final_iteration`, but no explicit follow-up marker.
- `execute_phase1_planning` always calls `git::require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths` in the enforcement loop, then runs `git::ensure_paths_unchanged` against baseline snapshots.
- Existing phase1 tests cover dirty-baseline behavior but not explicit follow-up semantics.
- Integration dirty-repo tests currently cover preflight-only cases, not multi-iteration phase behavior.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add explicit follow-up iteration metadata to phase invocation
File: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/mod.rs`

Add a new field so phase implementations do not infer follow-up semantics from prompt text.

```rust
pub struct PhaseInvocation<'a> {
    // ...existing fields...
    pub is_final_iteration: bool,
    pub is_followup_iteration: bool,
    pub allow_dirty_repo: bool,
    // ...existing fields...
}
```

### 2. Thread follow-up flag through run-one phase execution
File: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/run_one/phase_execution.rs`

Propagate `is_followup` into each phase invocation path.

```rust
for iteration_index in 1..=setup.iteration_settings.count {
    let is_followup = iteration_index > 1;
    let is_final_iteration = iteration_index == setup.iteration_settings.count;
    let allow_dirty = is_followup || setup.preexisting_dirty_allowed;

    execute_three_phase_iteration(
        // ...
        is_final_iteration,
        is_followup,
        allow_dirty,
        // ...
    )?;
}
```

Update all affected function signatures and `build_phase_invocation(...)` wiring:

```rust
fn build_phase_invocation<'a>(
    // ...
    is_final_iteration: bool,
    is_followup_iteration: bool,
    allow_dirty_repo: bool,
    // ...
) -> PhaseInvocation<'a> {
    PhaseInvocation {
        // ...
        is_final_iteration,
        is_followup_iteration,
        allow_dirty_repo,
        // ...
    }
}
```

### 3. Change phase1 follow-up enforcement behavior
File: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/phase1.rs`

Keep first-iteration behavior unchanged. For follow-up iterations, skip the global clean assertion call and instead validate that dirty paths remain constrained to:
- baseline dirty paths captured before Phase 1, and
- Phase 1 allowlisted bookkeeping paths.

Continue baseline snapshot enforcement in all cases.

Implementation shape:

```rust
fn enforce_followup_dirty_constraints(repo_root: &Path, allowed_refs: &[&str]) -> Result<(), GitError> {
    let current = git::status_paths(repo_root)?;
    if current.is_empty() {
        return Ok(());
    }

    if git::repo_dirty_only_allowed_paths(repo_root, allowed_refs)? {
        Ok(())
    } else {
        Err(GitError::DirtyRepo {
            details: "\n\nFollow-up Phase 1 violation: planning introduced dirty paths outside baseline and allowed queue/plan paths."
                .to_string(),
        })
    }
}
```

Use it in the loop:

```rust
let status = if ctx.is_followup_iteration {
    enforce_followup_dirty_constraints(&ctx.resolved.repo_root, &allowed_refs)
} else {
    git::require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths(&ctx.resolved.repo_root, false, &allowed_refs)
};

let snapshot_check = match status {
    Ok(()) => git::ensure_paths_unchanged(&ctx.resolved.repo_root, &baseline_snapshots)
        .map_err(|err| GitError::Other(err.context("baseline dirty path changed"))),
    Err(err) => Err(err),
};
```

This preserves plan-only protections while removing the follow-up global clean assertion.

### 4. Add/adjust unit tests for phase1 follow-up semantics
File: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/tests.rs`

Update all `PhaseInvocation` literals to include `is_followup_iteration`.

Add regression-focused tests:
- Follow-up planning with pre-existing dirty implementation file succeeds when runner only updates plan cache.
- Follow-up planning still fails when runner introduces a new disallowed dirty path (assert follow-up violation message).

Skeleton:

```rust
#[test]
fn phase1_followup_allows_preexisting_iteration_dirty_state() -> Result<()> {
    // setup repo + baseline dirty file from prior iteration
    // runner writes .ralph/cache/plans/RQ-0001.md only
    // invocation: is_followup_iteration=true, allow_dirty_repo=true
    // assert execute_phase1_planning succeeds
}

#[test]
fn phase1_followup_rejects_new_disallowed_dirty_paths() -> Result<()> {
    // setup baseline dirty path
    // runner creates new disallowed file during planning
    // invocation: is_followup_iteration=true, allow_dirty_repo=true
    // assert error mentions follow-up phase1 violation
}
```

### 5. Add integration regression for multi-iteration run
File: `crates/ralph/tests/git_repo_dirty_state_test.rs`

Add a test that configures `phases=3`, `iterations=2`, CI gate disabled, and uses a fake runner with call-count behavior:
- call 1 (phase1 iter1): write plan cache
- call 2 (phase2 iter1): create dirty implementation file
- call 4 (phase1 iter2): write plan cache again

Then run `ralph run one` and verify:
- execution reaches follow-up phase1 (e.g., plan cache content reflects iteration2 write), and
- stderr does **not** contain dirty-repo failure text from phase1 follow-up.

A final non-dirty-repo failure at later completion stages is acceptable for this regression as long as follow-up phase1 dirty-check failure is absent.

Suggested helper pattern inside test file:

```rust
fn configure_multi_iter_three_phase_runner(dir: &Path, runner_path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    // read .ralph/config.json as serde_json::Value
    // set agent.runner/model/codex_bin/phases=3/iterations=2/ci_gate_enabled=false/git_commit_push_enabled=false
    // write config back
    Ok(())
}
```

## Verification Commands (to run in Phase 2)
- `cargo test -p ralph commands::run::phases::tests`
- `cargo test -p ralph --test git_repo_dirty_state_test`

If failures point to additional follow-up iteration call sites, update them in the same change set (no compatibility shim paths).

## Constraints and Adaptation Rule
If file structure/names have changed since this plan was written, adapt the exact paths and helper names, but preserve these invariants:
1. Follow-up Phase 1 skips the global clean-repo assertion path.
2. Follow-up Phase 1 still enforces plan-only constraints.
3. Baseline dirty paths remain immutable during planning.
4. First-iteration behavior remains unchanged.
